articles.title: King and Queen Give Special Money in Easter Tradition
articles.summary: King Charles III and Queen Camilla went to a special church service in Wales to give away money. This happens every year before Easter and is called the Maundy service. It is a very old tradition that started over 800 years ago. The King and Queen gave gifts to 154 people who help others in their communities. Each person got two purses with special coins. One purse had silver coins that add up to 77 pennies because the King is 77 years old. The other purse had coins to remember the late Queen and a charity. The service was held at St Asaph Cathedral, and it was only the second time it happened in Wales. People at the church were happy and honored to have the King and Queen visit. The service reminds everyone to be humble and serve others, just like Jesus did at the Last Supper.

articles.title: Funeral Director Admits to Bad Actions
articles.summary: A funeral director named Robert Bush did very wrong things. He stopped 30 bodies from being buried properly and stole money that people gave to charities. This happened in Hull, England, from 2012 to 2024. Police found out and arrested him. In court, he said he was guilty of these crimes. He also gave families the wrong ashes and sold fake funeral plans. The judge said he will go to jail. Many families are very sad because of what he did. They will tell the court how they feel before he is sentenced. This story shows how important it is to be honest and kind when helping others.

articles.title: Storm Dave Brings Wind and Snow to the UK for Easter
articles.summary: A big storm named Dave is coming to the UK during Easter weekend. It will bring strong winds and snow, especially in the north. The Met Office, which is a weather group, has given yellow warnings. This means people should be careful because there might be damage, power cuts, and travel problems. Roads, trains, and ferries could be affected. Easter is usually a busy time for travel, so this storm might make things tricky. The storm is caused by low pressure and a fast jet stream from far away. While some places get bad weather, others might have rain or sunshine. It's important to stay safe and check weather updates.

articles.title: Countries Talk About Opening a Busy Sea Route
articles.summary: The United Kingdom is hosting an online meeting with about 30 countries to talk about reopening the Strait of Hormuz. This is a very important sea route in the Middle East where ships carry oil and gas. Iran has attacked some ships there, which has made it hard for ships to pass through and has caused fuel prices to go up around the world. The UK wants to find peaceful ways to make the strait safe again without getting into a bigger war. Other countries like France, Germany, and Japan are also involved. The meeting will discuss how to protect the ships and help lower energy costs for people everywhere.

articles.title: Supreme Court and Birthright Citizenship
articles.summary: The Supreme Court is hearing a case about birthright citizenship, which means if you are born in the United States, you are a citizen. This rule comes from the 14th Amendment to the Constitution and has been around for over 100 years. President Trump signed an order to try to end this rule, and now the justices are discussing it. They are looking at who gets to be a citizen and who makes the rules. Experts like Amy Howe and Amanda Frost are talking about the arguments in court. This is a big deal because it could change how citizenship works for many people.

articles.title: Wendy Red Star: Making Art About Her Native American Life
articles.summary: Wendy Red Star is an artist from the Crow Indian Reservation in Montana. She uses art like photos and sculptures to show her Native American identity and share stories from her childhood. Wendy grew up riding horses and going to events like Crow Fair with her family. Her grandma made special clothes called regalia for her. In college, she learned about a chief named Sits in the Middle of the Land who helped define Crow territory. This inspired her to set up teepees on campus to honor her heritage. Wendy believes art is very important because it helps people remember who they are. She says making art is a big part of her identity.
